languageDescription: Choose your preferred language. You can change this later in Settings.
privacyText: EVDx takes your privacy seriously. All vehicle data is stored locally on your device. We never send your diagnostic data to external servers. You have full control over your data and can export or delete it at any time.
privacyBullet1: All data stored locally on your device
privacyBullet2: No vehicle data sent to external servers
privacyBullet3: Full control to export or delete your data
privacyRequired: You must accept the privacy policy to continue
vehicleSetupDescription: Tell us about your electric vehicle so we can provide the best diagnostic experience.
adapterSetupDescription: Connect your OBD-II Bluetooth adapter to start reading vehicle data.
adapterRequirements: You'll need a compatible ELM327-based OBD-II Bluetooth adapter.

connectAdapterSteps.step1: Plug the adapter into your vehicle's OBD-II port

noAdapterFound: No adapter found. Make sure it's plugged in and powered.
connectionTestFailed: Connection test failed. Check your adapter.
tryDemoDescription: Explore EVDx with simulated vehicle data. No adapter needed.
demoDataNote: Demo mode uses simulated data. Connect a real adapter for live diagnostics.
getStartedDescription: You're all set! Start exploring your EV diagnostics.
setupCompleteDescription: Your EVDx is ready to use. You can always change settings later.

quickStartSteps.dashboard: View live vehicle data on the Dashboard
quickStartSteps.battery: Monitor battery health and cell status

skipSetupConfirm: Skip setup? You can complete it later in Settings.
obdExplanation: An OBD-II adapter plugs into your vehicle's diagnostic port and communicates with the car's computer systems via Bluetooth, allowing EVDx to read real-time data and diagnostic codes.
obdPortLocation: The OBD-II port is usually located under the dashboard on the driver's side, near the steering column. It's a 16-pin connector.
recommendedAdapter: Recommended: OBDLink MX+ or Vgate iCar Pro

troubleshooting.title: Connection Troubleshooting
troubleshooting.tip1: Make sure the adapter is firmly plugged into the OBD-II port
troubleshooting.tip2: Ensure vehicle ignition is ON (not just accessory mode)
troubleshooting.tip3: Check that Bluetooth is enabled on your phone
troubleshooting.tip4: Try turning Bluetooth off and on again
troubleshooting.tip6: Restart the adapter by unplugging and replugging it
troubleshooting.tip7: Check if another app is connected to the adapter
